GetOutlookMailMessages.note: Retrieves Outlook mail messages. Requires Outlook connection. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
GetOutlookMailMessages.description: Read mail messages from a desktop Outlook folder using the local Outlook profile.
GetOutlookMailMessages.use_when: User has Outlook installed and wants to fetch messages from a local mailbox folder. For Exchange Web Services use GetExchangeMailMessages; for Microsoft 365 Graph use plugin activities.

alternatives.use_instead_when: There is no locally-installed Outlook profile and you must hit Exchange Web Services directly.
The mailbox is non-Exchange (e.g. Gmail, generic IMAP) and there is no Outlook client to drive.
The server only exposes POP3 and you just need a one-shot download of new mail.

MoveOutlookMessage.note: Self-closing tag. Moves an Outlook mail message to a specified folder. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
MoveOutlookMessage.description: Move a desktop Outlook MailMessage to another folder in the same store.
MoveOutlookMessage.use_when: User wants to file an Outlook message to another folder (Archive, Done). For IMAP use MoveIMAPMailMessageToFolder; for Exchange use MoveMessageToFolder.

SendOutlookMail.note: Sends an email via Outlook. Requires Outlook connection. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
SendOutlookMail.description: Send an email through the local desktop Outlook profile.
SendOutlookMail.use_when: User has Outlook installed and wants to send through that profile. For Exchange use SendExchangeMail; for SMTP/Gmail use SendMailX.

alternatives.use_instead_when: There is no locally-installed Outlook profile and you must talk to Exchange Web Services directly.
You only have a generic SMTP relay (no Outlook client, no Exchange credentials).

OutlookApplicationCard.note: OutlookApplicationCard is a scope activity with ActivityAction body requiring Studio configuration.
OutlookApplicationCard.description: Modern Use Outlook scope (wizard-only) - pins an Outlook account for nested mail activities.
OutlookApplicationCard.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to configure Outlook Application Card through UiPath Studio's Outlook wizard.

ForEachEmailX.note: ForEachEmailX iterates over emails with a dual-argument ActivityAction body requiring Studio configuration.
ForEachEmailX.description: StudioX For Each Email iterator (wizard-only) over a mailbox folder.
ForEachEmailX.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to configure For Each Email X through StudioX's Outlook wizard. As an alternative iterate the output of GetOutlookMailMessages with ForEach.

ArchiveMailX.description: StudioX activity that moves an email to the configured Archive folder of the active mail account.
ArchiveMailX.use_when: User wants the StudioX 'Archive Email' button equivalent. For arbitrary moves use MoveOutlookMessage / MoveMessageToFolder.

CreateDraft.description: Create a draft email in the chosen mail account without sending it.
CreateDraft.use_when: User wants to stage an email for human review or later send. To send immediately use SendOutlookMail / SendExchangeMail / SendMailX.

CreateHtmlContent.description: Build an HTML body string from a template and variables for use as an email body.
CreateHtmlContent.use_when: User wants to compose a templated HTML email body before sending. Pair with SendOutlookMail / SendMailX.

DeleteImapMailMessage.description: Delete a MailMessage from an IMAP server.
DeleteImapMailMessage.use_when: User wants to remove a message via IMAP. For Outlook use DeleteOutlookMailMessage; for Exchange use DeleteMail; for Lotus Notes use DeleteLotusNotesMailMessage.

DeleteLotusNotesMailMessage.description: Delete a MailMessage from an IBM/HCL Lotus Notes mailbox.
DeleteLotusNotesMailMessage.use_when: User automates Lotus Notes mail and wants to delete a message. For other providers use DeleteImapMailMessage / DeleteOutlookMailMessage / DeleteMail.

DeleteMail.description: Delete a MailMessage through Exchange (EWS) using the active scope.
DeleteMail.use_when: User wants to delete an Exchange message inside an ExchangeScope. For Outlook desktop use DeleteOutlookMailMessage; for IMAP use DeleteImapMailMessage.

DeleteMailX.description: StudioX 'Delete Email' activity for the active mail account.
DeleteMailX.use_when: User wants the StudioX delete-email button equivalent. For provider-specific calls use DeleteOutlookMailMessage / DeleteImapMailMessage / DeleteMail.

DeleteOutlookMailMessage.description: Delete a MailMessage from the local desktop Outlook profile.
DeleteOutlookMailMessage.use_when: User wants to delete a message in desktop Outlook. For IMAP use DeleteImapMailMessage; for Exchange use DeleteMail.

ExchangeScope.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

ExchangeScope.description: Connect to Exchange Web Services (EWS) and run nested Exchange mail activities under that connection.
ExchangeScope.use_when: User wants EWS-based mail automation in a single scope. For Microsoft 365 Graph use the plugin scope; for desktop Outlook none - use the activities directly.

ForwardMailX.description: StudioX 'Forward Email' activity for the active mail account.
ForwardMailX.use_when: User wants to forward the current StudioX email. For provider-specific forwarding use the underlying Outlook/Exchange activities.

GetEmailById.description: Fetch a single mail message by its provider-specific identifier.
GetEmailById.use_when: User already knows the message ID (e.g. from a trigger) and wants the full MailMessage. For folder enumeration use the provider's Get*Mail* activity.

GetExchangeMailMessages.description: Read mail messages from an Exchange folder via Exchange Web Services (EWS).
GetExchangeMailMessages.use_when: User automates Exchange via EWS and wants to fetch messages. For desktop Outlook use GetOutlookMailMessages; for IMAP use GetPOP3MailMessages or an IMAP variant.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You want to use the locally-installed Outlook desktop client instead of talking to Exchange Web Services directly.
The server only exposes IMAP, or you need vendor-neutral folder access without Exchange credentials.
The server only exposes POP3 for a one-shot download.

GetLotusNotesMailMessages.description: Read mail messages from an IBM/HCL Lotus Notes mailbox.
GetLotusNotesMailMessages.use_when: User automates Lotus Notes mail. For other providers use GetOutlookMailMessages / GetExchangeMailMessages / GetPOP3MailMessages.

GetMailMessageFromFile.description: Load a saved .eml or .msg file from disk into a MailMessage variable.
GetMailMessageFromFile.use_when: User has previously saved an email to disk and wants to re-load it for processing. To save use SaveMail / SaveMailX.

GetPOP3MailMessages.description: Read mail messages from a POP3 server.
GetPOP3MailMessages.use_when: User must use POP3 (legacy). Prefer IMAP or Exchange where available; use GetExchangeMailMessages or GetOutlookMailMessages instead.

alternatives.use_instead_when: The server supports IMAP and you need folder navigation or server-side flags rather than a one-shot POP3 download.
You can use the Exchange Web Services endpoint directly for richer querying than POP3.
You want to read from a logged-in Outlook desktop profile rather than poll a POP3 server.

MarkMailAsReadX.description: StudioX 'Mark Email as Read' activity for the active mail account.
MarkMailAsReadX.use_when: User wants the StudioX mark-as-read button equivalent. For provider-specific control use MarkOutlookMailAsRead.

MarkOutlookMailAsRead.description: Mark a desktop Outlook MailMessage as read or unread.
MarkOutlookMailAsRead.use_when: User wants to flip the read flag on an Outlook message. For StudioX use MarkMailAsReadX.

MoveIMAPMailMessageToFolder.description: Move a MailMessage to another folder on an IMAP server.
MoveIMAPMailMessageToFolder.use_when: User automates IMAP and wants to file a message. For Outlook use MoveOutlookMessage; for Exchange use MoveMessageToFolder; for StudioX use MoveMailX.

MoveLotusNotesMailMessage.description: Move a Lotus Notes MailMessage to another mailbox folder.
MoveLotusNotesMailMessage.use_when: User automates Lotus Notes and wants to file a message.

MoveMailX.description: StudioX 'Move Email' activity for the active mail account.
MoveMailX.use_when: User wants the StudioX move-email button equivalent. For provider-specific calls use MoveOutlookMessage / MoveIMAPMailMessageToFolder / MoveMessageToFolder.

MoveMessageToFolder.description: Move an Exchange MailMessage to another folder via EWS inside an ExchangeScope.
MoveMessageToFolder.use_when: User automates Exchange via EWS. For Outlook use MoveOutlookMessage; for IMAP use MoveIMAPMailMessageToFolder.

NewIMAPEmailReceivedTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when a new email arrives on an IMAP server.
NewIMAPEmailReceivedTrigger.use_when: User wants an event-driven workflow that reacts to new IMAP mail. For Outlook event triggers use OutlookMailMessagesTrigger.

OutlookMailMessagesTrigger.description: Trigger that fires when a new email arrives in a desktop Outlook folder.
OutlookMailMessagesTrigger.use_when: User wants an event-driven workflow that reacts to new Outlook mail. For IMAP use NewIMAPEmailReceivedTrigger.

ReplyToMailX.description: StudioX 'Reply to Email' activity for the active mail account.
ReplyToMailX.use_when: User wants the StudioX reply button equivalent. For provider-specific replies use ReplyToOutlookMailMessage.

ReplyToOutlookMailMessage.description: Reply (or Reply All) to a desktop Outlook MailMessage with new body and optional attachments.
ReplyToOutlookMailMessage.use_when: User wants to compose a reply to an Outlook message. For Exchange/IMAP, reply via the underlying provider's send activity with appropriate headers.

SaveExchangeAttachements.description: Save all attachments from an Exchange MailMessage to a folder on disk.
SaveExchangeAttachements.use_when: User wants to extract attachments from an Exchange message. For other providers use SaveMailAttachmentsX.

SaveMail.description: Save a MailMessage to disk as an .eml/.msg file.
SaveMail.use_when: User wants to persist an email for archival or later re-import. For attachments only use SaveMailAttachmentsX; to re-load use GetMailMessageFromFile.

SaveMailAttachmentsX.description: StudioX activity that saves all attachments of the current email to a folder.
SaveMailAttachmentsX.use_when: User wants the StudioX 'Save Attachments' button equivalent. For Exchange specifically use SaveExchangeAttachements.

SaveMailX.description: StudioX 'Save Email' activity that writes the current email to disk.
SaveMailX.use_when: User wants the StudioX save-email button equivalent. For provider-agnostic saving use SaveMail.

SaveOutlookMailMessage.description: Save a desktop Outlook MailMessage to disk as a .msg file.
SaveOutlookMailMessage.use_when: User wants to persist an Outlook message in native .msg format. For provider-agnostic .eml use SaveMail.

SendCalendarInviteX.description: StudioX activity that sends a calendar invitation through the active mail account.
SendCalendarInviteX.use_when: User wants to send a meeting invite, not a plain email. For ordinary mail use SendOutlookMail / SendMailX.

SendExchangeMail.description: Send an email through Exchange Web Services (EWS) inside an ExchangeScope.
SendExchangeMail.use_when: User wants to send via EWS. For desktop Outlook use SendOutlookMail; for SMTP use SendMailX.

alternatives.use_instead_when: There is a logged-in Outlook desktop client; let it deliver the mail so it follows local Outlook rules.
You do not have Exchange credentials and only need to push through a generic SMTP relay.

SendLotusNotesMailMessage.description: Send an email through an IBM/HCL Lotus Notes session.
SendLotusNotesMailMessage.use_when: User automates Lotus Notes outbound mail.

SendMailX.description: Send an email via SMTP (configurable host/port/credentials).
SendMailX.use_when: User wants to send through a generic SMTP server (Gmail SMTP, custom relay). For Outlook use SendOutlookMail; for Exchange use SendExchangeMail.

SetOutlookMailCategories.description: Apply or replace one or more Outlook category labels on a desktop Outlook MailMessage.
SetOutlookMailCategories.use_when: User wants to tag an Outlook message with category colours/labels.
